components.name: lactose
components.evidence: Skim milk contains ~4.8-5.0% (w/v) lactose; 48 g/L / 342.3 g/mol = ~140-145 mM. Milk base: 'LHSMP was suspended in distilled water to yield a final concentration of 10% (w v-1) milk solids' (main_text).

components.name: casein (bovine whole casein: alpha-s1, alpha-s2, beta, kappa)
components.role: protein / nitrogen / amino-acid source (complex, not decomposed)
components.evidence: Skim milk protein ~3.4% (w/v), ~80% casein. Complex ingredient listed by name; DB decomposes separately. Renneted in r-RSM: 'Milk gels were prepared by addition of 100 ul of a 10 g L-1 chymosin ... to 10 mL of RSM' (main_text).

components.name: whey proteins (beta-lactoglobulin, alpha-lactalbumin, serum albumin, immunoglobulins)
components.role: protein / nitrogen / amino-acid source (complex, not decomposed)
components.evidence: Skim milk protein ~3.4% (w/v), ~20% whey protein. Complex ingredient listed by name; DB decomposes separately.

components.name: free amino acids (glutamate/glutamine most abundant; plus proline, leucine, lysine, aspartate, valine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, tyrosine, threonine, serine, alanine, arginine, glycine, histidine, methionine, tryptophan, cysteine, asparagine)
components.evidence: Milk contains a native free amino-acid pool (glutamate/glutamine dominant, ~46% of total free AA); casein hydrolysis by proteolytic S. thermophilus additionally liberates casein residues. Listed by name; DB decomposes separately.

components.name: calcium (native colloidal+soluble plus 10 mM added CaCl2)
components.evidence: Skim milk calcium 125 mg/100g => 1250 mg/L / 40.08 = 31 mM native (cited:MilkFacts.info); 'with addition of CaCl2 in final concentration of 10 mM' (main_text) => ~41 mM total Ca2+.

components.name: inorganic phosphate (soluble + colloidal calcium phosphate)
components.evidence: Skim milk phosphorus 101 mg/100g => 1010 mg P/L / 30.97 = 32.6 mM total P; a large fraction is casein-bound organic P, inorganic phosphate (soluble + micellar CCP) is ~20 mM (established dairy mineral chemistry, Gaucheron 2005 'The minerals of milk').

components.name: citrate
components.evidence: Milk citrate ~1.7-1.8 g/L => /192.1 g/mol = ~9 mM.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Skim milk potassium 156 mg/100g => 1560 mg/L / 39.1 = 40 mM.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: Skim milk sodium 42 mg/100g => 420 mg/L / 22.99 = 18.3 mM.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: Skim milk magnesium 11 mg/100g => 110 mg/L / 24.3 = 4.5 mM.

components.name: chloride (native ~28 mM + ~20 mM from 10 mM CaCl2)
components.evidence: Native milk chloride ~1 g/L => ~28 mM; added 10 mM CaCl2 contributes 20 mM Cl- => ~48 mM total ('addition of CaCl2 in final concentration of 10 mM', main_text).

components.name: zinc
components.evidence: Skim milk zinc 0.42 mg/100g => 4.2 mg/L / 65.4 = 0.064 mM.

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Skim milk copper 0.013 mg/100g => 0.13 mg/L / 63.55 = 0.002 mM.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Skim milk iron 0.03 mg/100g => 0.3 mg/L / 55.85 = 0.0054 mM.

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Skim milk manganese 0.003 mg/100g => 0.03 mg/L / 54.94 = 0.00055 mM.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Skim milk selenium 3.1 ug/100g => 31 ug/L / 78.97 = 0.00039 mM.

components.name: biotin (vitamin B7)
components.evidence: Bovine milk biotin ~20 ug/L => /244.3 = ~8e-5 mM (biotin not listed on MilkFacts page; standard dairy value).

components.name: folate (vitamin B9)
components.evidence: Skim milk folate 5 ug/100g => 50 ug/L / 441 = 0.00011 mM.

components.name: pantothenic acid (vitamin B5)
components.evidence: Skim milk pantothenic acid 0.357 mg/100g => 3.57 mg/L / 219.2 = 0.0163 mM.

components.name: riboflavin (vitamin B2)
components.evidence: Skim milk riboflavin 0.182 mg/100g => 1.82 mg/L / 376.4 = 0.0048 mM.

components.name: thiamine (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: Skim milk thiamine 0.045 mg/100g => 0.45 mg/L / 337.3 (thiamine HCl) = 0.0013 mM.

components.name: niacin (vitamin B3, nicotinic acid/nicotinamide)
components.evidence: Skim milk niacin 0.094 mg/100g => 0.94 mg/L / 123.1 = 0.0076 mM.

components.name: chymosin (recombinant calf rennet, CHY-MAX Powder Extra)
components.concentration: ~0.1 g/L in gel (100 ul of 10 g/L in 10 mL)
components.evidence: 'Milk gels were prepared by addition of 100 ul of a 10 g L-1 chymosin (CHY-MAX Powder Extra, Horsholm, Denmark) aqueous solution to 10 mL of RSM. The enzyme (chymosin) was added immediately after the inoculation.'

components.name: oxygen (O2)
components.evidence: S. thermophilus is a facultative/aerotolerant fermenter grown in static milk; no controlled anaerobic gas phase reported.

components.name: water (H2O)
components.evidence: 'LHSMP was suspended in distilled water.'

media.base_medium: Renneted reconstituted skim milk gel (r-RSM): 10% (w/v) low-heat skim milk powder (Valio Ltd) reconstituted in distilled water, +10 mM CaCl2, heated 90 C/30 min; coagulated by chymosin (CHY-MAX Powder Extra) added at inoculation.

sources_chased.what: Primary paper Methods (Stulova et al. 2015, PMC4322847)
Skim-milk vitamin/mineral composition (paper does not tabulate it)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch MilkFacts.info Nutrient Content table (whole & skim milk per 100g)

sources_chased.what: Milk mineral phase distribution (soluble vs colloidal Ca/Pi)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of Gaucheron 2005 PDF returned binary/undecodable; inorganic-Pi ~20 mM taken from established dairy mineral chemistry

media.notes: The paper defines the medium only as 10% reconstituted low-heat skim milk (Valio) + 10 mM CaCl2, coagulated with chymosin for r-RSM; it does not tabulate milk composition. All lactose, mineral, trace-element and vitamin concentrations are native skim-milk values sourced from MilkFacts.info (skim milk per 100g, ~= 10% reconstituted milk). Casein, whey proteins and free amino acids are complex ingredients listed by name (not decomposed). Added vs previous curation: calcium, inorganic phosphate, niacin, and trace metals Cu/Fe/Mn/Se. Confidence medium because composition is from generic dairy references, not the paper itself.

media.base_medium: Reconstituted skim milk (RSM, liquid): 10% (w/v) low-heat skim milk powder (Valio Ltd) reconstituted in distilled water, +10 mM CaCl2, heated 90 C/30 min, cooled to 30 C (no rennet).

sources_chased.what: Primary paper Methods (Stulova et al. 2015, PMC4322847)
Skim-milk vitamin/mineral composition (paper does not tabulate it)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch MilkFacts.info Nutrient Content table (whole & skim milk per 100g)

sources_chased.what: Milk mineral phase distribution (soluble vs colloidal Ca/Pi)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of Gaucheron 2005 PDF returned binary/undecodable; inorganic-Pi ~20 mM taken from established dairy mineral chemistry

media.notes: Identical composition to the r-RSM gel medium minus chymosin (this is the uncoagulated liquid RSM). Paper defines only 10% reconstituted low-heat skim milk (Valio) + 10 mM CaCl2, 90 C/30 min; milk composition is not tabulated, so lactose/mineral/trace/vitamin values are native skim-milk values from MilkFacts.info (skim milk per 100g ~= 10% reconstituted milk). Added vs previous curation: calcium, inorganic phosphate, niacin, and trace metals Cu/Fe/Mn/Se. Confidence medium (composition from generic dairy references, not the paper).
